methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
ANYbotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparables universe of nine private robotics firms at similar stage, supplemented by one public sector peer (277810.KQ). The peer set includes 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat, among others.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, yields $95M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $1.1B, reflecting a wide distribution from $300M to $10.0B. The dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in market positioning and capital efficiency within the sector at this stage.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $81M to $1.2B, with a median point estimate of $585M. The wide range reflects structural uncertainty: stage-progression assumes ANYbotics follows typical funding step patterns but relies on limited historical data for this company; peer-median is anchored by six data points but spans nearly two orders of magnitude, indicating that comparable robotics firms command vastly different valuations even within Series B. Key sensitivities include customer traction relative to peer benchmarks, capital efficiency, and near-term financing environment. The median estimate of $585M sits meaningfully below peer median, suggesting either valuation discount relative to peers or selective application of the peer set.
